questions.question: When does Notion charge you for your subscription?

options.text: At the end of your billing interval
options.explanation: Notion charges you upfront at the beginning of your billing interval, not at the end.

options.text: At the start of your billing interval
options.explanation: You always pay for Notion at the start of your billing interval.

options.text: On a random day each month
options.explanation: Billing is always scheduled based on the interval you choose (monthly or yearly), not randomly.

options.text: When you upgrade your plan
options.explanation: Upgrading triggers a new charge, but the base subscription is always billed at the start of the interval.

questions.question: If you add a member to your workspace mid-billing cycle, how are they charged?

options.text: You are charged immediately for the full month/year
options.explanation: You are charged for the portion of the interval they are in the workspace; proration applies.

options.text: You are charged immediately for the remainder of the interval
options.explanation: We prorate charges for added members for the remainder of the current billing interval.

options.text: You are not charged until the next billing cycle
options.explanation: Adding members is a billable action; the charge is prorated for the time they are in the workspace.

options.text: The charge is applied to the previous invoice as a credit
options.explanation: There is no credit applied; the charge is added to cover the new member's access.

questions.question: How can you collaborate with someone in Notion without incurring charges for an additional member seat?

options.text: Add them to your workspace as a member
options.explanation: Adding members to a paid workspace is a billable action and will incur charges.

options.text: Invite them as a guest to specific pages
options.explanation: You can invite guests to specific pages instead of adding them as members to avoid billable charges.

options.text: Create a new workspace for them
options.explanation: Creating a new workspace would still require a paid seat for that workspace.

options.text: Send them an email invitation only
options.explanation: To collaborate, they must have access; adding them as a guest to a page is the cost-free way.

questions.question: When you remove a member from your workspace mid-cycle, what happens to the paid seat?

options.text: The seat is released immediately and your bill decreases now
options.explanation: Decreases in billing for removals are reflected at your next billing cycle, not immediately.

options.text: The seat is held until the end of your current billing interval
options.explanation: You have access to the paid seat until the end of the interval and can reassign it to another person.

options.text: The seat is permanently deleted and cannot be reassigned
options.explanation: The seat remains available for reassignment until the interval ends.

options.text: You are refunded immediately for the unused time
options.explanation: There is no proration or refund for removing members mid-cycle.

questions.question: Which statement is true regarding yearly vs. monthly billing?

options.text: Monthly billing costs less than yearly billing
options.explanation: Yearly billing costs less than monthly billing.

options.text: Yearly plans renew on the same calendar day every year
options.explanation: If you pay yearly, your plan will renew on the same calendar day you first purchased it.

options.text: Monthly plans offer more control over your budget
options.explanation: Monthly plans are recommended if you expect your team size to fluctuate often or want more control over your budget.

options.text: You can change from yearly to monthly at any time without penalty
options.explanation: While you *can* change the interval, the text notes that changing from yearly to monthly takes effect the day the annual cycle ends.

questions.question: You are on a monthly plan and decide to switch to a yearly plan. What happens to the remaining time on your monthly cycle?

options.text: The remaining time is refunded to your credit card
options.explanation: The remaining time is subtracted from your annual payment, not refunded.

options.text: The remaining time carries over to the next year
options.explanation: The remaining time is subtracted from the new yearly price.

options.text: The remaining time is forfeited
options.explanation: The remaining time is used to offset the cost of the new yearly plan.

options.text: The remaining time is added to the end of your new yearly cycle
options.explanation: The remaining time is subtracted from the annual payment to prorate the cost.

questions.question: If you have multiple paid Notion workspaces, how are they billed?

options.text: They are billed together as one account
options.explanation: You are billed for them separately based on the number of members in each.

options.text: They are billed based on the total number of members across all workspaces
options.explanation: They are billed separately based on members in each individual workspace.

options.text: You only pay for the workspace with the most members
options.explanation: Each workspace is billed based on its own specific member count.

options.text: You can have different billing intervals per workspace
options.explanation: The text states you can have different billing intervals per workspace.

questions.question: Where can you view how many paid seats a specific workspace has?

options.text: Settings > Billing > Workspaces
options.explanation: You view this information in Settings > People.

options.text: Sidebar > Billing
options.explanation: You navigate to Settings in the sidebar first, then to People.

options.text: Settings > People
options.explanation: Go to Settings in your sidebar → People to view paid seats and members.

options.text: Billing tab in the top header
options.explanation: The People tab is located under Settings.

questions.question: Who is authorized to view and manage plans and billing details?

options.text: All members of the workspace
options.explanation: Only workspace owners can view and manage plans and billing.

options.text: All editors and viewers
options.explanation: Only workspace owners have access to billing management.

options.text: Only workspace owners
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that only workspace owners can view and manage plans and billing.

options.text: Administrators only
options.explanation: The specific term used in the text is 'workspace owners'.

questions.question: What happens if your paid workspace is downgraded to a free plan due to an unpaid invoice?

options.text: You are immediately banned from Notion
options.explanation: The system automatically downgrades the account after several attempts to collect payment, but you aren't banned immediately.

options.text: It is automatically downgraded after several attempts to collect payment
options.explanation: Our system will automatically downgrade a paid account after several attempts are made to collect payment.

options.text: You can no longer log into your account
options.explanation: You can still log in and re-upgrade via the Upgrade tab in Settings.

options.text: The unpaid invoice is cleared automatically
options.explanation: The unpaid invoice causes the downgrade, which must be resolved manually by re-upgrading.

questions.question: To organize a team (e.g., engineering) with granular permissions without creating a separate workspace, what should you do?

options.text: Create a new workspace for that team
options.explanation: The article suggests avoiding multiple workspaces by using permissions and groups instead.

options.text: Disable Workspace Access and grant access per page
options.explanation: You can disable Workspace Access and grant specific levels of access per page to specific groups.

options.text: Add all engineers to a 'Design' group
options.explanation: While groups are mentioned, the specific solution to avoid a separate workspace is disabling workspace access and managing permissions at the page level.

options.text: Merge all workspaces into one
options.explanation: The goal is to avoid multiple workspaces entirely by using permissions.

questions.question: When changing your billing interval from monthly to yearly, what is the effective date of the new cycle?

options.text: The day you click 'Confirm changes'
options.explanation: The new billing cycle starts the next year on the same calendar day you made the change.

options.text: The day your annual cycle ends
options.explanation: This applies to changing from yearly to monthly. Changing monthly to yearly takes effect immediately regarding the new plan, but the renewal date is next year.

options.text: The next annual renewal date
options.explanation: The new billing cycle will take effect the next year on the same calendar day you made the change.

options.text: The end of the current month
options.explanation: The new cycle is tied to the calendar day of purchase, not the month end.

questions.question: What is the primary reason to choose monthly billing over yearly billing?

options.text: Monthly billing costs less
options.explanation: Yearly billing costs less. Monthly is chosen for budget control.

options.text: To allow for mid-year proration adjustments
options.explanation: Proration happens in both monthly and yearly; monthly just allows more frequent adjustments.

options.text: If you expect your team size to fluctuate often
options.explanation: If you expect team size to fluctuate often, or you want more control over your budget, monthly is recommended.

options.text: To avoid paying for unused time at the end of the year
options.explanation: Both models handle unused time/proration; monthly simply happens more frequently.

questions.question: How can you ensure that an invited person is added as a guest rather than a member?

options.text: Select 'Upgrade' on the invitation screen
options.explanation: Selecting 'Upgrade' turns them into a member. You must do the opposite to keep them as a guest.

options.text: Hover over their email address and select 'Skip for now'
options.explanation: The article instructs to hover over the email and select 'Skip for now' to ensure they are added as a guest.

options.text: Add them to a group first
options.explanation: Groups are for permissions, not for the initial invite method to avoid member status.

options.text: Invite them via email link instead of the Share menu
options.explanation: The method described is specific to the Share menu and the 'Skip for now' prompt.

questions.question: Plans in Notion apply to which of the following?

options.text: Your entire Notion account
options.explanation: Plans apply to a single workspace and do not cover your whole account.

options.text: Specific pages only
options.explanation: Plans apply to the workspace level, not individual pages.

options.text: A single workspace
options.explanation: The article states that plans only apply to a single workspace.

options.text: Groups of users
options.explanation: Plans are per workspace, and groups are a permission tool within that workspace.
